key_insight: A single “cliff token” can trigger a model’s shift from correct mathematical reasoning toward failure, and identifying it enables recovery and targeted improvement.
engineering_relevance: For LLM reasoning systems, token-level failure detection can guide decoding, training, and debugging strategies that improve reliability on math benchmarks.
method: Detect cliff tokens by looking for a significant drop in token-wise potential under an adaptive, local threshold using a one-sided two-proportion z-test.
Classify cliffs into deterministic, uncertain, and sampled-off types using greedy choice and token entropy.
Validate with Cliff-DPO: single-token preference optimization at cliff positions to improve downstream reasoning.
evidence_signal: Across seven models and three benchmarks (GSM1K, MATH500, AIME 2025), deleting the first cliff token and resampling recovered pass@64 to 1.0, while keeping it limited recovery to 0.71–1.00.
watch_item: The method depends on token-level potential/entropy estimates and benchmark-specific training (Cliff-DPO trained on GSM8K), so robustness and transfer beyond these settings may need further validation.
paper_title: Cliff Tokens: Identifying Single-Token Failure Triggers in LLM Mathematical Reasoning
